meta_title: For Students: project management to Solve can't debug code (Intermediate Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome can't debug code. A Intermediate-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Project Management for Students: How to Debug AI Code
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with bugs. Learn professional project management strategies using Antigravity and Claude Code to accelerate your student development journey.
content.answer_block: Debugging isn't a lack of talent; it's a lack of process. By implementing structured project management, you break complex logic into modular, testable units. Using Antigravity for architectural visualization and Claude Code for iterative refinement, you transform chaotic troubleshooting into a predictable, high-velocity engineering workflow that ships stable software.
content.intro:
The screen glows, the terminal spits out a wall of red text, and the silence in your room feels heavy. You’ve been staring at the same function for three hours, and the logic that felt so clear this morning has dissolved into a labyrinth of broken dependencies and silent failures. Every student developer knows this friction; it is the crucible where amateur hobbyists are forged into professional engineers.

But here is the secret: elite engineers don't 'fix' bugs—they manage the complexity that creates them. By shifting from reactive hacking to proactive project management, you reclaim control over your codebase. Today, we bridge the gap between confusion and mastery using the industry's most powerful AI-assisted tools.

steps.title: Define the Failure State
steps.description: Before you write a single line of code, document the exact failure. Break the problem into inputs, expected outputs, and the current reality.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this code module and create a state-transition map identifying where the data flow diverges from the expected path.
steps.claude_code_command: claude read ./src/logic.py --prompt 'Trace the state of the variables at line 42 during execution.'

steps.title: Isolate the Logic Domain
steps.description: Don't debug the whole app. Extract the failing function into a test harness to isolate it from external dependencies.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a minimal unit test scaffold for this specific function to isolate it from the main application state.

steps.title: Architectural Visualization
steps.description: Use Antigravity to visualize how your data moves. If you can see the architecture, the bug becomes an anomaly in the structure.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Visualize the dependency graph for this component and highlight potential circular references causing the runtime crash.

steps.title: Iterative Hypothesis Testing
steps.description: Apply the scientific method. Make one change, verify, and document. Never change two variables at once.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Propose three potential fixes for this null pointer exception, ranked by impact and risk level.

steps.title: Systematize the Resolution
steps.description: Once fixed, create a regression test to ensure the bug never returns. Update your project management board to reflect the learned lesson.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a regression test suite that covers this edge case and integrates into the existing CI pipeline.
steps.claude_code_command: claude commit -m 'feat: resolved runtime crash with regression test'

pro_tips: Treat your terminal history as a project diary; if you can't repeat the fix, you haven't actually solved the problem.
Use 'Rubber Ducking' with Claude Code—explain the bug in plain English to the model; it often identifies the flaw before you finish typing.
If a bug takes longer than 60 minutes to resolve, stop coding and start whiteboarding the data flow manually.
Keep your project scope small; 80% of bugs originate from 'feature creep' that you didn't plan for properly.
Always commit your failing test case before you attempt the fix—this creates a clear 'Before and After' for your portfolio.
content.next_steps: Now that you have debugged your first major module, apply this exact workflow to your next feature branch. Document your process in a README.md file to showcase your professional methodology to potential employers.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools, the process, and the mindset to stop guessing and start building production-grade software. Stop letting bugs dictate your progress and start leading your code. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.

acceptedAnswer.text: Before you write a single line of code, document the exact failure. Break the problem into inputs, expected outputs, and the current reality.
Don't debug the whole app. Extract the failing function into a test harness to isolate it from external dependencies.
Use Antigravity to visualize how your data moves. If you can see the architecture, the bug becomes an anomaly in the structure.
Apply the scientific method. Make one change, verify, and document. Never change two variables at once.
Once fixed, create a regression test to ensure the bug never returns. Update your project management board to reflect the learned lesson.
